topic: Application and External AI Agent CLI Hot-Update and Version Control System
context: We researched the third reference library, cc-switch, to design our AI Agent CLI Control Center (for tools like Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode, and Hermes).
options_considered: Implement a basic shell execution dashboard that runs user-provided upgrade command strings.

Implement a structured version detection and one-click cloud update manager using: (1) PATH-based probe runners to detect local CLI versions, (2) registry query adapters for NPM, PyPI, and GitHub APIs to get remote versions, and (3) a silent native sub-process builder to execute installer scripts securely.
chosen_option: Implement a structured version detection and one-click cloud update manager with registry adapters and silent process execution.
reasoning: Scanning version output (running '{cli} --version') in PATH is the most reliable way to verify local CLI presence without assumptions.
Using specific API adapters for NPM (registry.npmjs.org), PyPI, and GitHub Releases is the industry standard to retrieve remote versions safely without HTML scraping.

Executing updates via silent native background tasks (using tokio::process in Tauri or child_process in Electron) with CREATE_NO_WINDOW on Windows keeps the UI smooth and provides clean error logging back to the user.
tradeoffs: Querying public registries might fail under restricted offline corporate environments; we will support offline caching and network timeouts gracefully.
impact_scope: CLI Manager module (Rust backend or Node service layer)
UI Settings and Tool Dashboard (showing update badges and install buttons)
future_risks: Varying command syntax formats (e.g. some CLIs return 'version is x.y' while others return 'x.y') require robust regex parsing to prevent extraction crashes.
